items.headline: U.S. Futures Dip Following Strong Quarter-End Rally, Investors Eye Fed Commentary and Economic Data
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are trading lower on Wednesday, starting July in the red, despite major indices closing out their strongest second quarter and first half since 2020 on Tuesday. Market sentiment remains cautiously optimistic after a tech-led surge, but investors are now weighing a busy day of economic reports and remarks from Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh for clues on potential interest rate movements. Concerns about a possible rate hike continue to loom.

items.headline: Bloom Energy Corporation
items.summary: The stock surged in pre-market trading, leading gainers.

items.headline: ServiceNow, Inc.
items.summary: ServiceNow saw strong pre-market gains following an upgrade by Guggenheim to a 'Buy' rating from 'Neutral'.

items.headline: Regal Rexnord Corporation
items.summary: The company was among the top pre-market gainers.

items.headline: Mueller Industries, Inc.
items.summary: Mueller Industries experienced a significant decline in pre-market trading.

items.headline: Micron Technology
items.summary: The chipmaker's stock was down 6% in pre-market trading after a strategic customer support deal with General Motors.

items.headline: Fed Chair Warsh to Speak at ECB Forum Amid Rate Hike Speculation
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is scheduled to speak at the European Central Bank's annual forum in Sintra, Portugal, today. Markets will be closely watching his remarks for insights into the future path of interest rates, especially after the Fed's June meeting indicated a hawkish shift with half of the officials anticipating at least one rate hike in 2026.

items.headline: Key Economic Reports Due Today: ADP Employment and ISM Manufacturing
items.summary: Investors are awaiting crucial economic data releases today, including the June ADP employment report and the ISM Manufacturing PMI. These reports will provide fresh insights into the health of the U.S. labor market and manufacturing sector, influencing expectations for monetary policy.

items.headline: Tech and AI Lead as Market Shifts from Cyclicals
items.summary: Technology and growth stocks, particularly those in the semiconductor and AI sectors, continue to drive market performance, extending a multi-quarter trend. This highlights an ongoing sector rotation, with capital flowing out of more mature cyclical sectors like industrials and financials, which lagged on Tuesday.

items.headline: General Mills Reports Q4 Fiscal 2026 Results in Line with Expectations
items.summary: General Mills ($GIS) announced its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 adjusted results, which met company expectations. The company reported net sales up 1% to $4.6 billion and adjusted diluted earnings per share (EPS) up 27% to $0.95.

items.headline: USMCA Trade Deal Extension in Doubt as Deadline Passes
items.summary: The United States will not extend its trade pact with Canada and Mexico by today's deadline, instead initiating further talks. The fate of the $2 trillion North American trade deal is now in doubt, raising concerns about prolonged uncertainty that could depress investment in the neighboring countries.

items.headline: Crude Oil Prices Decline Amid Easing Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Crude oil prices are continuing to decline today, with Brent crude falling to $71.62 per barrel and WTI crude to $68.88. This softening in oil prices comes amid easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, contributing to a positive global market trend.
